analysis.problem: Lack of trust in the community hinders the effective response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient engagement and communication with local communities, leading to mistrust and misinformation
leverage_points: World Health Organization (WHO) and local health authorities

proposed_actions.action: Implement a community-based outreach and education program to build trust and provide accurate information about Ebola
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve a 20% increase in community-reported Ebola cases within 6 months

proposed_actions.action: Establish a regional cooperation framework to share best practices and coordinate responses to Ebola outbreaks
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Conduct at least 2 regional workshops within the next 12 months, with participation from at least 5 countries

evidence: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: how trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domain of 'Planet' (healthy biosphere) and 'Human' (dignity for all), as it affects the well-being and livelihoods of individuals and communities, and highlights the need for effective global health governance and cooperation
analysis.summary: The Ebola outbreak in the DRC is hindered by a lack of trust in the community, which can be addressed through community-based outreach and education programs, as well as regional cooperation and coordination. This issue has significant implications for global health security and the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals.

raw_items.title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
raw_items.summary:
Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak. Read Full Story on UN News

raw_items.title: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: how trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
raw_items.summary:
In Ebola-stricken Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost, humanitarians said on Tuesday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: how trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

raw_items.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
raw_items.summary:
Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday. Read Full Story on UN News

raw_items.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
raw_items.summary:
The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission. Read Full Story on UN News
